task_completion.description: Create a new Resource Dictionary named 'Theme.xaml' in the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer pane in Visual Studio shows a new file named 'Theme.xaml' under the 'SystemMonitorDashboard' project.

task_completion.description: Refactor and move the styles and brushes into Theme.xaml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor tab for 'Theme.xaml' is visible, showing a <ResourceDictionary> containing SolidColorBrush and Style definitions (e.g., BrandPrimaryBrush, BrandSecondaryBrush, CardStyle).

task_completion.description: Remove the hardcoded styles and brushes from the Window.Resources section of MainWindow.xaml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor tab for 'MainWindow.xaml' is visible, and its <Window.Resources> section is either empty or no longer contains the brushes and styles that were moved to Theme.xaml.

task_completion.description: Merge 'Theme.xaml' into the application resources in App.xaml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code editor tab for 'App.xaml' is visible and contains a <ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries> block with a <ResourceDictionary Source="Theme.xaml" /> entry.

task_completion.description: Ensure the solution builds without StaticResource resolution errors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output window or Error List in Visual Studio displays a successful build message (e.g., 'Build: 1 succeeded') with no XAML StaticResource resolution errors shown.

integrity.description: The agent used the Visual Studio 2022 GUI to perform the refactoring.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Visual Studio 2022 IDE being actively used (Solution Explorer, XAML editor, Output window), rather than terminal commands or a basic text editor.

integrity.description: The Theme.xaml file is genuinely integrated into the WPF project.
integrity.visual_evidence: Solution Explorer shows 'Theme.xaml' properly nested within the 'SystemMonitorDashboard' project hierarchy, indicating it was added to the project file and not just opened as a loose file.

integrity.description: The agent actually triggered a build to verify the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Output window shows recent build logs, or the Visual Studio status bar indicates 'Build succeeded', proving the agent didn't just edit the text and skip verification.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target project name is verified as 'SystemMonitorDashboard'. The expected resource keys that should be refactored into Theme.xaml are 'BrandPrimaryBrush', 'BrandSecondaryBrush', and 'CardStyle'.
